div.h1: DML Write Path — INSERT Data Flow
div.p: Every write is buffered through the pool. No component bypasses it.
addr opcode p1 p2 p3 comment

0 OP_INIT 0 1 0 Start 1 OP_OPEN_WRITE 0 5 3 cursor=0, root=5 2 OP_NEW_ROWID 0 1 0 cursor=0 → r1 3 OP_STRING8 0 2 0 'Alice' → r2 4 OP_INTEGER 30 3 0 30 → r3 5 OP_NOT_NULL 2 0 0 name NOT NULL 6 OP_MAKE_RECORD 2 2 4 r2..r3 → record r4 7 OP_INSERT 0 4 1 cursor=0, rec=r4, key=r1 8 OP_CLOSE 0 0 0 9 OP_HALT 0 0 0 Done
No component below the VM ever calls pwrite() or fsync() directly. All page modifications happen in-memory on frame->data buffers owned by the Buffer Pool. The modified bytes reach disk only through:

ol.li: Eviction — evict_frame() writes dirty frame before reusing it
Explicit flush — pool_flush_all() writes all dirty frames + fsync

div.p: The transaction layer (M9) hooks into dirty-marking to journal the original page bytes before the dirty flag is set, ensuring crash recovery can restore pre-transaction state.

ol.li: Look up "users" in CompileContext → root_page=5, col_count=3, schema

div.p: r0: [scratch] r1: VAL_INTEGER i=1 ← rowid (from OP_NEW_ROWID) r2: VAL_STRING "Alice" ← from OP_STRING8 r3: VAL_INTEGER i=30 ← from OP_INTEGER r4: VAL_BLOB [record] ← from OP_MAKE_RECORD

pc=0: OP_INIT → pc=1 pc=1: OP_OPEN_WRITE → table_cursor_create() pc=2: OP_NEW_ROWID → cur->max_rowid() + 1 → r1 pc=3: OP_STRING8 → pool string → r2 pc=4: OP_INTEGER → 30 → r3 pc=5: OP_NOT_NULL → r2 != NULL ✓ pc=6: OP_MAKE_RECORD→ serialize r2,r3 → r4 pc=7: OP_INSERT → cur->insert(r1, r4) pc=8: OP_CLOSE → cur->close() pc=9: OP_HALT → halt_code=0

Serial type header: header_size = 4 (varint: 0x04) serial_type[0] = 23 (TEXT, len=5: 5×2+13) serial_type[1] = 4 (32-bit int) Value body: "Alice" (5 bytes, raw UTF-8) 0x0000001E (30 as 4-byte big-endian) Total record: 4 + 5 + 4 = 13 bytes
[varint: payload_size=13] → 0x0D (1 byte) [varint: rowid=1] → 0x01 (1 byte) [record body: 13 bytes] Total cell: 1 + 1 + 13 = 15 bytes

ol.li: Collect all cells + overflow cell, sorted

page_table_lookup(page_id) HIT → pin_count++, lru_move_to_head(), return frame->data MISS → get_free_frame() or evict_frame() → pread(fd, frame->data, 4096, offset) → page_table_insert(), lru_push_head() → return frame->data
IF dirty AND NOT already dirty: a. IF txn_journal_page hook set (M9): → journal original page BEFORE marking b. frame->is_dirty = true

div.p: Same journal hook + set is_dirty flag

p.strong: Frame struct (32 bytes metadata + 4096 bytes data)

div.p: Frame { page_id: 5 ← which DB page pin_count: 0 ← safe to evict when 0 is_dirty: true ← MUST write back before reuse lru_prev: 2 ← doubly-linked LRU chain lru_next: -1 (tail) data: →[4096 bytes at data_block + i×4096] }

ol.li: Scan LRU from tail → find pin_count==0
IF is_dirty: a. txn_journal_page() hook (M9) b. fsync(journal_fd) ← BEFORE db write! c. pwrite(fd, data, 4096, page_id×4096) d. stat_disk_writes++ e. is_dirty = false

div.p: FOR each frame WHERE is_dirty: pwrite(fd, data, 4096, page_id × 4096) is_dirty = false fsync(fd) ← barrier: all pages durable

Offset 0×4096: Page 0 (sqlite_master root) Offset 1×4096: Page 1 ... Offset 5×4096: Page 5 (users table root) ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ Hdr: type=0x0D cell_count=1 │ │ Ptr[0] → offset 4081 │ │ │ │ [free space: 4081 - 10 bytes] │ │ │ │ @4081: cell(rowid=1, "Alice", 30)│ └──────────────────────────────────┘
